fields.name: runtime birthday party on unit damage flags!~
runtime birthday party on unit death flags!~
runtime birthday party on unit post-death damage flags!~
runtime birthday party on unit destruction flags!~
body vitality multiplier#modifies how much body vitality actors will have
shield vitality multiplier#modifies how much shield vitality actors will have
Birthday Party
fields.definition: A birthday party allows you to celebrate the death of a given unit with special effects and damage consequences. One or more birthday parties can be defined for any skull and multiple birthday parties can be defined for a given unit.

fields.name: skull^#the skull which enables this birthday party
special damage types{special death types}#the birthday party is only applied if the damage matches one of these special damage types. if none are set, all are valid
effect damage reporting type#the damage type to use for this birthday party's effects
Unit Filters
fields.definition: The following fields define filters for which unit(s) this birthday applies to. If no units are specified, all units matching the team flags are affected.

fields.name: team flags#the team(s) this birthday party is applied to. if none are set, all are valid
unit#the primary unit this birthday party is applied to
secondary unit#the secondary unit this birthday party is applied to
Consequences
fields.definition: The following fields define the consequences of this birthday party if it passes the filters above

fields.name: dropped grenade count#0 defaults to 1 if a grenade type is specified
custom stimulus#a stimulus which can result in a custom character definition being used if defined in a character's "stimuli responses" block
stimulus duration:s#random duration within the bounds will be used if min<max
enemy-of team#this skull is applied to all teams which are an enemy of this team
rate of fire scale#this scalar is applied to the rate of fire of the firing pattern the actor is using when firing a burst
damage falloff radius scale#scales the distance beyond which area-of-effect damage begins falling off
damage cutoff radius scale#scales the distance beyond which area-of-effect damage is not dealt
player unit constant sound volume override#overrides the "constant sound volume" defined in the player's unit definition

player unit noise adjustment#adjusts AI perception of noises made by the player's unit up or down by this many sound volume levels. this functions the same way the "object noise adjustment" in equipment definitions does
hearing distance scale#scales the "hearing distance" in characters' "perception properties"
Recession
fields.definition: These scalars are applied to weapons directly owned by players (i.e. not vehicle weapons).

fields.name: rounds per shot scale#scales the number of rounds removed from a magazine from each shot fired, drains magazine but still fires if total rounds exceed loaded rounds
heat per shot scale#scales the amount of heat generated from each shot fired
age per shot scale#scales how much a weapon ages from each shot fired
age per kill scale#scales how much a weapon ages with each kill (if the "weapon ages with each kill" flag is set, e.g. for the energy sword)
age charge drain scale#scales how much a weapon ages while a trigger is charged (e.g. for the plasma pistol)
age external scale#scales how much a weapon ages from external sources (e.g. for the gravity hammer's explosive effects)
actor types#the actor types these scalars apply to while this skull is enabled
Object Remap
fields.definition:
These skulls use the same object remap concepts as multiplayer. You can define weapon and vehicle sets as well as weights for the builtin random set in a multiplayer object type list tag and specify which sets to use when the skull is active. You can choose whether to load all objects referenced by the multiplayer object type list (will be loaded by all levels regardless of whether the skull is enabled) or to only resolve references to objects which are intrinsically loaded by the level.

If the "force load objects" flag is set, ALL objects in the 'object types' block and ALL of their dependencies will be loaded on EVERY level! Do NOT set that flag unless you've constrained your referenced objects down to a reasonable set!

fields.name: damage scale while sprinting#scales the damage applied to sprinting players while this skull is active
allied teams#all specified teams will be allied with one another unless allied with the player
indestructible vehicles#if empty, all vehicles are indestructible
Awarded Vitality Computation
fields.definition: The "give" portion of this skull converts realized damage to awarded vitality.

fields.name: damage -> awarded vitality scale#the fraction of the actual damage dealt which is converted to awarded vitality
maximum awarded shield vitality#the maximum fractional shield vitality that can be awarded
Damage Potential Computation
fields.definition:
The "take" portion of this skull is applied when firing a weapon or throwing a grenade, before any damage has been dealt. As such, it operates on damage potential: an approximation of how much damage the fired / thrown projectile could deal. Damage potential is constant for a given projectile. If the projectile's damage effect's damage bounds are variable, the mean is used.

Note that damage potential can exceed the realizable damage for a given target: a frag grenade may have a damage potential of 160, but its target may only have 30 vitality. Using that projectile against that target will be a net negative unless the awarded vitality scale significantly exceeds the self damage scale.

The damage bounds in the "self damage effect" are not actually used, as the damage amount is computed based on the scaled damage potential. The damage effect provides any other consequences (e.g. camera shake) rather than the damage amount, but its damage bounds must be non-zero.

fields.name: projectile fired damage potential type flags#for fired weapons
projectile thrown damage potential type flags#for thrown grenades
projectile conical spread bias:[0, 1]#if the projectile has a "conical spread" block defined, self damage will be computed based on this fraction of its conical projections missing

projectile impact distance bias:[0, 1]#if the projectile has the "damage scales based on distance" flag set, self damage will be computed based on the damage it would deal at this fraction of its damage range
projectile detonation distance bias:[0, 1]#self damage will be computed as if the projectile detonates this fraction of its detonation damage effect's cutoff radius away from its target
incident threshold scale#scales the number of traitorous incidents required for a friendly team to become non-friendly, note that the threshold is rounded to a whole number
incident decay timer scale#scales how long it takes for a friendly team to forget about a traitorous incident
lava damage reporting type#the damage type to use for lava-induced damage
lava damage threshold:s#damage commences after touching lava for this long
lava damage frequency:s#damage applies at this interval after reaching the threshold
projectile creation location override#controls where the player's weapon projectiles are created while the third person skull is active
throw aim origin override#controls where the player's grenades are created while the third person skull is active

options: celebrate on damage#this birthday party's consequences are invoked whenever the unit takes damage while alive
celebrate on death#this birthday party's consequences are invoked when the unit dies
celebrate on post-death damage#this birthday party's consequences are invoked whenever the unit takes damage while dead
celebrate on destruction#this birthday party's consequences are invoked when the unit is destroyed
only celebrate if damage owner is not self
only celebrate if damage owner is enemy
only celebrate if damage owner is player
only celebrate if damage owner is at maximum shield vitality
only celebrate if damage owner is at maximum body vitality
destroy unit after death#destroy the unit when it reaches the last frame in its death animation
do not scale melee damage#these scalars are applied to all area-of-effect damage (which includes ai melees) by default
affects weapons#if set, weapons can only be picked up if affiliated with the same faction as the player
affects equipment#if set, equipment can only be picked up if affiliated with the same faction as the player
affects grenades#if set, grenades can only be picked up if affiliated with the same faction as the player
affects factionless objects#if set, objects not affiliated with a faction may not be picked up
disable perception positional attenuation#disables attenuation of AI perception based on the actor's position relative to the target
disable perception actor mode attenuation#disables attenuation of AI perception based on the actor's mode (alert, asleep, etc.)
disable perception line of sight attenuation#disables attenuation of AI perception based on the actor's line of sight to the target
silent#ai will not respond to this sound
loud#ai can hear this sound at any range
force load objects#if set, all object references in the multiplayer object type list will be loaded
suppress death from self damage#if set, self damage can reduce vitality to zero but never kill
impact#the projectile's "impact damage" is incorporated into the computed damage potential
detonation#the projectile's "detonation damage" is incorporated into the computed damage potential
stun shield on touch#if set, the unit's shield will be stunned (stop recharging) upon touching lava until damage is applied
stun body on touch#if set, the unit's body will be stunned (stop recharging) upon touching lava until damage is applied
use observer aim line#weapon projectiles spawn at a position offset along the aim line starting at the observer position
use deterministic camera aim line#position offset along the aim line starting at the deterministic position (which does not take into account collisions with level geo)
use weapon origin#weapon projectiles always spawn at the weapon origin when on foot
same as first person#projectile spawning uses what the unit and weapon tags say in first person
use observer aim line#weapon projectiles spawn at a position offset along the aim line starting at the observer position
use deterministic camera aim line#position offset along the aim line starting at the deterministic position (which does not take into account collisions with level geo)
throw release position#use the object's position at the moment of release, which is visually consistent with what the character is doing but probably doesn't feel great
same as first person#use the head camera position (as in first-person)
